description: OCS message about movement of light rail vehicles

partitionkey.description: Key used to balance messages across multiple shards

dataSource.description: The comm type from which positional data was obtained. `avi` indicates information was obtained from an AVI read via a wayside device. `nvl` ("non-vital logic") indicates information was obtained from a track circuit. `gps` indicates information was obtained from a GPS
dataSource.$comment: TODO: (1) Is it safe for this to be an enum? (2) Is it best to drop the `ci` suffix as RTR does?

consist.description: Full consist, ie car numbers of this vehicle, as represented internally in OCS

consistDisplay.description: Full consist, ie car numbers of this vehicle, as displayed physically

speed.description: Speed of vehicle, if dataSource is gps
speed.$comment: TODO: RTR uses an explicit 'unreported' value here. Should we just be optional / nullable?

heading.description: Direction the vehicle is pointing, measured in degrees from Magnetic North. Only provided with GPS data source.
heading.$comment: TODO: RTR uses an explicit 'unreported' value here. Should we just be optional / nullable?

orientation.description: Whether the 'rear' or 'front' of each train is facing forward for each train in the consist. B means backward, A means forward. Only provided with AVI data source.
